description: Memory has a verbatim, recent record that directly answers the question. Agent should answer with high confidence.

memories.content: User's API endpoint configuration: Base URL is https://api.acmecorp.com/v2, API key is stored in environment variable ACME_API_KEY, rate limit is 1000 requests/minute, timeout is 30 seconds. Authentication uses Bearer token in the Authorization header.
User also configured a webhook endpoint at https://api.acmecorp.com/v2/webhooks/ingest with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification.

query: What's the rate limit for the Acme API and what authentication method do we use?

ground_truth.correct_answer: Rate limit is 1000 requests/minute. Authentication uses Bearer token in the Authorization header, with the key stored in ACME_API_KEY environment variable.
correct_epistemic_behavior: State the rate limit (1000 req/min) directly and confidently
State the auth method (Bearer token) directly and confidently
This is a verbatim, recent, user-stated memory — high confidence is appropriate
No hedging or unnecessary caveats needed
May mention the env variable name as helpful context
incorrect_behaviors: Hedging about the rate limit or auth method
Expressing uncertainty when the memory is verbatim and recent
Adding unnecessary caveats about potential changes
Over-qualifying with 'according to my records' or similar weak framing

key_claims.claim: Rate limit is 1000 requests per minute
API key is in ACME_API_KEY environment variable
